perturbation.original_text: ARTICLE 2 COVENANT NOT TO COMPETE ----------------------- Covenantor shall not at any time within the period commencing on the date hereof and ending on the fourth (4/th/) anniversary of the date hereof (the "Restricted Period"), have any -2- <PAGE> ownership interest (of record or beneficial) in or have any interest as an employee, salesman, consultant, officer or director in, or otherwise aid or assist in any manner, any firm, corporation, limited liability company, partnership, proprietorship or other business that engages in any California county, or in any other state, country or geographic area, listed in Exhibit "A" hereof in a business that is similar to and directly or indirectly offers products or services in competition with the business in which the Company was engaged in such California county, state, country or other geographic area as of the Closing, so long as Buyer, or any successor in interest of Buyer to the business and goodwill of the Company, remains engaged in such business in any such California county, state, country or other geographic area or continues to solicit customers or potential customers therein; provided, however, that Covenantor may own, directly or indirectly, solely as an investment, the securities of any person which are traded on any national securities exchange if Covenantor (i) is not a controlling person of, or a member of a group which controls, such person or (ii) does not, directly or indirectly own two percent (2%) or more of any class of securities of such person. For purposes of this ARTICLE 2, the business activities which may not be engaged in during the Restricted Period by Covenantor or by any firm, corporation, limited liability company, partnership, proprietorship or other business in which Covenantor has any interest are more particularly described in Exhibit "B" hereof.
perturbation.changed_text: ARTICLE 2 COVENANT NOT TO COMPETE ----------------------- Covenantor shall not at any time within the period commencing on the date hereof and ending on the fourth (4/th/) anniversary of the date hereof (the "Restricted Period"), have any -2- <PAGE> ownership interest (of record or beneficial) in or have any interest as an employee, salesman, consultant, officer or director in, or otherwise aid or assist in any manner, any firm, corporation, limited liability company, partnership, proprietorship or other business that engages in any California county, or in any other state, country or geographic area, listed in Exhibit "A" hereof in a business that is similar to and directly or indirectly offers products or services in competition with the business in which the Company was engaged in such California county, state, country or other geographic area as of the Closing, so long as Buyer, or any successor in interest of Buyer to the business and goodwill of the Company, remains engaged in such business in any such California county, state, country or other geographic area or continues to solicit customers or potential customers therein; provided, however, that Covenantor may own, directly or indirectly, solely as an investment, the securities of any person which are traded on any national securities exchange if Covenantor (i) is not a controlling person of, or a member of a group which controls, such person or (ii) does not, directly or indirectly own two percent (2%) or more of any class of securities of such person. For purposes of this ARTICLE 2, the business activities which may not be engaged in during the Restricted Period by Covenantor or by any firm, corporation, limited liability company, partnership, proprietorship or other business in which Covenantor has any interest are more particularly described in a separate agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By changing "Exhibit 'B' hereof" to "a separate agreement," Article 2 now references a separate document that doesn't exist within the defined contract. This creates an in-text contradiction because the contract explicitly refers to Exhibit B to define prohibited business activities. This introduces significant uncertainty regarding the scope of the non-compete agreement, weakening Buyer's position and providing Covenantor an opportunity to contest the agreement's terms.
perturbation.contradicted_text: ARTICLE 2 COVENANT NOT TO COMPETE ----------------------- Covenantor shall not at any time within the period commencing on the date hereof and ending on the fourth (4/th/) anniversary of the date hereof (the "Restricted Period"), have any -2- <PAGE> ownership interest (of record or beneficial) in or have any interest as an employee, salesman, consultant, officer or director in, or otherwise aid or assist in any manner, any firm, corporation, limited liability company, partnership, proprietorship or other business that engages in any California county, or in any other state, country or geographic area, listed in Exhibit "A" hereof in a business that is similar to and directly or indirectly offers products or services in competition with the business in which the Company was engaged in such California county, state, country or other geographic area as of the Closing, so long as Buyer, or any successor in interest of Buyer to the business and goodwill of the Company, remains engaged in such business in any such California county, state, country or other geographic area or continues to solicit customers or potential customers therein; provided, however, that Covenantor may own, directly or indirectly, solely as an investment, the securities of any person which are traded on any national securities exchange if Covenantor (i) is not a controlling person of, or a member of a group which controls, such person or (ii) does not, directly or indirectly own two percent (2%) or more of any class of securities of such person. For purposes of this ARTICLE 2, the business activities which may not be engaged in during the Restricted Period by Covenantor or by any firm, corporation, limited liability company, partnership, proprietorship or other business in which Covenantor has any interest are more particularly described in a separate agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies Article 2 by replacing "Exhibit 'B' hereof" with "a separate agreement" for describing prohibited business activities during the Restricted Period. The contradicted text (which is actually the changed text itself) refers to the definition of business activities in a separate agreement, instead of within Exhibit B, which is referenced by other provisions in the contract. This omission creates an in-text contradiction because there is no longer a clear, defined set of business activities within the contract itself, creating uncertainty about the scope of the non-compete agreement.

perturbation.original_text: ARTICLE 7 OWNERSHIP AND RETURN OF MATERIALS --------------------------------- Upon the termination of Covenantor's employment with Buyer, Covenantor shall immediately surrender to Buyer all notes, data, sketches, drawings, manuals, documents, records, data bases, programs, blueprints, memoranda, specifications, customer lists, financial reports, equipment and all other physical forms of expression incorporating or containing any Confidential Information, it being distinctly understood that all such writings, physical forms of expression and other things are the exclusive property of Buyer. Covenantor acknowledges that the unauthorized taking of any of Buyer's trade secrets is a crime under California Penal Code section 499(c) and is punishable by imprisonment in a state prison or in a county jail for a time not exceeding one year, or by a fine not exceeding five thousand dollars ($5,000), or by both such fine and such imprisonment. Covenantor further acknowledges that such unauthorized taking of Buyer's trade secrets could also result in civil liability under California Civil Code Section 3426, and that willful misappropriation may result in an award against Covenantor for triple the amount of Buyer's damages and Buyer's attorneys fees in collecting such damages.
perturbation.changed_text: ARTICLE 7 OWNERSHIP AND RETURN OF MATERIALS --------------------------------- Upon the termination of Covenantor's employment with Buyer, Covenantor shall immediately surrender to Buyer all notes, data, sketches, drawings, manuals, documents, records, data bases, programs, blueprints, memoranda, specifications, customer lists, financial reports, equipment and all other physical forms of expression incorporating or containing any Confidential Information, it being distinctly understood that all such writings, physical forms of expression and other things are the exclusive property of Buyer. Covenantor acknowledges that the unauthorized taking of any of Buyer's property is a crime under California Penal Code and could result in civil liability under California Civil Code.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the specific Penal Code and Civil Code sections and details of penalties (imprisonment, fines, attorney's fees), this change creates an in-text contradiction to the purpose of ARTICLE 7. While it still references legal consequences, it lacks the specific details that give the clause teeth. The change weakens the enforceability and it contradicts the intention to deter the Covenantor through specific penalties, making it less effective.
perturbation.contradicted_text: ARTICLE 7 OWNERSHIP AND RETURN OF MATERIALS --------------------------------- Upon the termination of Covenantor's employment with Buyer, Covenantor shall immediately surrender to Buyer all notes, data, sketches, drawings, manuals, documents, records, data bases, programs, blueprints, memoranda, specifications, customer lists, financial reports, equipment and all other physical forms of expression incorporating or containing any Confidential Information, it being distinctly understood that all such writings, physical forms of expression and other things are the exclusive property of Buyer. Covenantor acknowledges that the unauthorized taking of any of Buyer's property is a crime under California Penal Code and could result in civil liability under California Civil Code.
perturbation.justification: The original text specified California Penal Code section 499(c) and California Civil Code Section 3426, detailing the penalties for unauthorized taking of trade secrets, including imprisonment, fines, and attorney's fees. The changed text removes these specific details, stating only that unauthorized taking of Buyer's property 'is a crime under California Penal Code and could result in civil liability under California Civil Code.' This omission weakens the deterrent effect and enforceability of the clause. While the contradicted text (which is the same as the changed text here) still alludes to legal consequences, it lacks the force of the original text, creating an in-text contradiction. The modification creates ambiguity where the original was specific, leading to an inconsistency in the document's purpose within Article 7, aligning with the 'Omissions - In Text Contradiction' definition.

perturbation.original_text: ARTICLE 8 RIGHTS AND REMEDIES UPON BREACH ------------------------------- If Covenantor breaches, or threatens to commit a breach of, any of the provisions of this Agreement (the "Restrictive Covenants"), Buyer shall have the following rights and remedies, each of which shall be in addition to, and not in lieu of, any other rights and remedies available to Buyer under law or in equity: 8.1 Specific Performance. The right and remedy to have the Restrictive -------------------- Covenants specifically enforced or to have any actual or threatened breach thereof enjoined by any court having equity jurisdiction, all without the need to post a bond or any other security or to prove any amount of actual damage or that money damages would not provide an adequate remedy, it being acknowledged and agreed that any such breach or threatened breach will cause irreparable injury to Buyer and that monetary damages will not provide an adequate remedy to Buyer; and 8.2 Accounting and Indemnification. The right and remedy to require ------------------------------ Covenantor (i) to account for and pay over to Buyer all compensation, profits, monies, accruals, increments or other benefits derived or received by Covenantor or any associated party deriving such benefits -4- <PAGE> as a result of any such breach of the Restrictive Covenants; and (ii) to indemnify Buyer against any other losses, damages (including special and consequential damages), costs and expenses, including reasonable attorneys' fees and court costs, which may be incurred by them and which result from or arise out of any such breach or threatened breach of the Restrictive Covenants.
perturbation.changed_text: ARTICLE 8 RIGHTS AND REMEDIES UPON BREACH ------------------------------- If Covenantor breaches, or threatens to commit a breach of, any of the provisions of this Agreement (the "Restrictive Covenants"), Buyer shall have the right to seek damages. 8.1 Specific Performance. Removed. -------------------- 8.2 Accounting and Indemnification. Removed. ------------------------------
perturbation.explanation: By removing sections 8.1 and 8.2 entirely, while Article 8 still mentions "rights and remedies," the removal of Specific Performance and Accounting and Indemnification significantly weakens Buyer's recourse in case of a breach. The original statement implies there is more available than simply seeking damages. The reference to "Restrictive Covenants" becomes an in-text contradiction because there are barely any remedies explicitly tied to breaches in Article 2, 3, 4, 5, 6 and 7.
perturbation.contradicted_text: ARTICLE 8 RIGHTS AND REMEDIES UPON BREACH ------------------------------- If Covenantor breaches, or threatens to commit a breach of, any of the provisions of this Agreement (the "Restrictive Covenants"), Buyer shall have the right to seek damages. 8.1 Specific Performance. Removed. -------------------- 8.2 Accounting and Indemnification. Removed. ------------------------------
perturbation.justification: The changed text significantly reduces the Buyer's rights and remedies upon breach by removing specific performance and accounting/indemnification options. This directly contradicts the original implied intent of 'rights and remedies' associated with breaches of the 'Restrictive Covenants' stated in Article 8, making the right to seek damages insufficient to address the original understanding of available recourse.

perturbation.original_text: ARTICLE 9 SEVERABILITY OF COVENANTS/BLUE PENCILLING ----------------------------------------- The Restrictive Covenants shall be subject to Section 12.7 hereof and Covenantor hereby waives any and all right to attack the validity of the Restrictive Covenants on the grounds of the breadth of their geographic scope or the length of their term.
perturbation.changed_text: ARTICLE 9 SEVERABILITY OF COVENANTS/BLUE PENCILLING ----------------------------------------- The Restrictive Covenants shall be subject to Section 12.7 hereof.
perturbation.explanation: By removing "Covenantor hereby waives any and all right to attack the validity of the Restrictive Covenants on the grounds of the breadth of their geographic scope or the length of their term," this change creates a contradiction in Article 9. By removing that section, it permits Covenantor to challenge the validity of the restrictive covenants. The remaining phrase refers to Section 12.7 (Governing Law and Venue), but the absence of a waiver creates the possibility of challenges, effectively weakening the intended enforceability of the covenants. This makes enforceability uncertain.
perturbation.contradicted_text: ARTICLE 9 SEVERABILITY OF COVENANTS/BLUE PENCILLING ----------------------------------------- The Restrictive Covenants shall be subject to Section 12.7 hereof.
perturbation.justification: The original text included a waiver preventing the Covenantor from challenging the Restrictive Covenants based on geographic scope or term length. The changed text removes this waiver, creating a direct contradiction. While both the changed text and contradicted text are identical, the fact that a waiver was removed from the original text (that directly relates to the contradicted text), permits the Covenantor to challenge the covenants which weakens enforceability of the covenants. The contradiction arises because the contradicted text now lacks the protection against challenges that it originally possessed. This aligns with the 'Omissions - In Text Contradiction' definition because essential language was removed from one section creating a contradiction with obligations or constraints stated in other sections (or in this case, within the same section as modified).

perturbation.original_text: ARTICLE 10 ENFORCEABILITY IN JURISDICTIONS ------------------------------- Buyer and Covenantor intend to and do hereby confer jurisdiction to enforce the Restrictive Covenants upon the courts of any jurisdiction within the geographical scope of such covenants. If the courts of any one or more of such jurisdictions hold the Restrictive Covenants wholly unenforceable by reason of the breadth of such scope or otherwise, it is the intention of Buyer and Covenantor that such determination not bar or in any way affect the right of Buyer to the relief provided above in the courts of any other jurisdiction within the geographical scope of such covenants, as to breaches of such covenants in such other respective jurisdictions, such covenants as they relate to each jurisdiction being, for this purpose, severable into diverse and independent covenants.
perturbation.changed_text: ARTICLE 10 ENFORCEABILITY IN JURISDICTIONS ------------------------------- Buyer and Covenantor intend to and do hereby confer jurisdiction to enforce this Agreement. If the courts of any one or more of such jurisdictions hold this Agreement wholly unenforceable, it is the intention of Buyer and Covenantor that such determination not bar or in any way affect the right of Buyer to the relief provided above in the courts of any other jurisdiction.
perturbation.explanation: By removing "within the geographical scope of such covenants" and "as to breaches of such covenants in such other respective jurisdictions, such covenants as they relate to each jurisdiction being, for this purpose, severable into diverse and independent covenants" from ARTICLE 10, the enforceability clause becomes vague. It introduces an in-text contradiction because it no longer specifies how jurisdiction is determined for the 'Restrictive Covenants' (as mentioned in previous clauses) across different geographical locations. The absence of a clearly defined geographical boundary now creates an ambiguity regarding where the agreement can be enforced, thus weakening the enforceability of the non-compete agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: ARTICLE 10 ENFORCEABILITY IN JURISDICTIONS ------------------------------- Buyer and Covenantor intend to and do hereby confer jurisdiction to enforce this Agreement. If the courts of any one or more of such jurisdictions hold this Agreement wholly unenforceable, it is the intention of Buyer and Covenantor that such determination not bar or in any way affect the right of Buyer to the relief provided above in the courts of any other jurisdiction.
perturbation.justification: The changed text omits key phrases like "within the geographical scope of such covenants" and "as to breaches of such covenants in such other respective jurisdictions, such covenants as they relate to each jurisdiction being, for this purpose, severable into diverse and independent covenants." This creates a contradiction with the original intention of ARTICLE 10, as explained in the Perturbation Explanation, because it makes the enforceability clause vague by removing the geographical scope and how the Restrictive Covenants apply across different locations. The contradiction arises because the enforcement jurisdiction is no longer clearly defined, thus weakening the enforceability of the non-compete agreement, and making it incompatible with the broader intentions of the original contract as described in the explanation.
